mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0% within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential construction financing indicate a synchronized credit and construction boom, which historically precedes broad equity outperformance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical episodes where both series rise concurrently for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 95.0, capacity constraints tighten and input cost inflation accelerates, compressing corporate margins and forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production remains above 95.0 for consecutive months while S&P 500 20-day forward returns average positive, contradicting the margin compression thesis.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands the earnings denominator for public corporations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth is positive but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, breaking the earnings-growth linkage.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month annualized inflation rate derived from the CPI exceeding 3.0 percent forces market expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: CPI annualized inflation exceeds 3.0% for two consecutive quarters while S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive, invalidating the risk premium channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle, which historically sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to yield positive 20-day equity returns in more than 55% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surpassing 600 thousand units while real GDP expands confirms a synchronized macroeconomic upswing, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week through improved corporate sentiment and cash flow expectations.
failure_condition: Both housing starts and GDP rise concurrently but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in over 50% of instances, disproving the sentiment-driven channel.
